prerequisites: A SurveyMonkey account. To collect data the app and the granting account must have access to the surveys and responses you want to replicate.
An app registered in the SurveyMonkey developer portal (MY APPS). Registering creates a draft app with credentials and an access token valid against your own account for 90 days during development.
Your registered app's client_id and client_secret (shown in the app's Settings), plus an OAuth access token for the account whose surveys you want to read.
A start_date: surveys and responses created on or after this date are replicated.
Optionally, the specific survey_ids to limit replication to, and the origin (data center) matching your account region.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 access token
authMethods.summary: Supply credentials.access_token, credentials.client_id, and credentials.client_secret. The connector calls the API with the header 'Authorization: bearer <access_token>'. For a private app accessing only your own account, use the access token generated when you registered the app (found in the app Settings under MY APPS). For other accounts, run the OAuth authorization-code flow: send the user to https://api.surveymonkey.com/oauth/authorize, then exchange the returned code at https://api.surveymonkey.com/oauth/token using your client_id and client_secret to obtain the access token. Access tokens do not currently expire but can be revoked by the user.

setupSteps.title: Register an app and get credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the SurveyMonkey developer portal and create an app under MY APPS. Registering creates a draft app with a client_id, client_secret, and an access token. The draft token works against your own account for 90 days during development. Copy the client_id and client_secret from the app's Settings page.

setupSteps.title: Obtain an access token
setupSteps.body: For a private app that only reads your own SurveyMonkey account, use the access token shown in the app's Settings. To access another user's account, run the OAuth 2.0 flow: redirect the user to https://api.surveymonkey.com/oauth/authorize with your client_id, redirect_uri, and response_type=code; then POST the returned code to https://api.surveymonkey.com/oauth/token with client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, and grant_type=authorization_code to receive the long-lived access token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: In the connector configuration, set credentials.access_token, credentials.client_id, and credentials.client_secret to the values from your registered app. The connector authenticates each request with 'Authorization: bearer <access_token>' over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Set replication scope
setupSteps.body: Provide the required start_date so only surveys and responses on or after that date are replicated. Optionally set survey_ids to restrict replication to specific surveys, and set origin to the data center for your account region (US default, EU, or CA), which selects the matching API base URL (api.surveymonkey.com, api.eu.surveymonkey.com, or api.surveymonkey.ca).

sources.title: SurveyMonkey API v3 documentation (authentication, OAuth, access tokens)
